articles.headline: Where must the Netherlands improve after Japan draw?

images.name: Where must the Netherlands improve after Japan draw?

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: Yamal on Spain bench vs. Cape Verde
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Spain v Cape Verde Pedri Kevin Pina [1296x729]
Moreno: Signing Cucurella would make sense for Real Madrid
images.caption: Moreno: Signing Cucurella would make sense for Real Madrid

articles.headline: Senegal's Nicolas Jackson must use the FIFA World Cup to impress Chelsea, and Xabi Alonso
articles.description: Senegal striker Nicolas Jackson may actually fit new Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso's vision for strikers better than many may initially assume. The FIFA World Cup is a chance to prove that.

images.name: Nicolas Jackson [1296x729]
images.caption: Nicolas Jackson was neither here nor there with Bayern Munich while on loan, and can use the World Cup with Senegal to really up his stocks with new Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso.
images.credit: Cory Knowlton/ISI Photos/ISI Photos via Getty Images

images.name: Chelsea's Nicolas Jackson [1296x729]
images.caption: Nicolas Jackson's time with Chelsea has been largely disappointing for all concerned, but he is an Alonso striker more than most would think.

images.name: Marcotti: Chelsea missing out on Europe is a blessing for Alonso
images.caption: Gab Marcotti explains why Chelsea missing out on a European spot could benefit Xabi Alonso.

articles.headline: Every team's championship, group odds for the 2026 World Cup
articles.description: Odds for all 48 teams to advance from their group through the knockout stage and to win the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Felix Nmecha [576x324]
images.caption: Felix Nmecha and Germany open strong against Curacao.
images.alt: Felix Nmecha #23 of Germany celebrates his goal by pretending to take off his crown during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E match.

images.name: Vinicius Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: With a 1-1 draw, Brazil saw their odds to win the World Cup lengthen from +950 to 11-1.
images.alt: Vinicius Junior of Brazil runs with the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium on June 13, 2026 in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
images.credit: Shaun Botterill - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Yamale for WC Odds file (260529) [1296x729]
images.caption: Spain's World Cup fortunes are tied to the health of Lamine Yamal's left leg.
images.alt: Spain's Lamine Yamal, right, scores his side third goal during the UEFA Nations League quarterfinal second leg match between the Netherlands and Spain at Mestalla stadium in Valencia, Spain, Sunday, March 23, 2025.

images.name: Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappe and France are looking to win their third World Cup.

article.headline: Colombia leading Greece 1-0 at halftime
article.story: BELO HORIZONTE, Brazil -- Colombia started its first World Cup campaign in 16 years in dazzling fashion, beating Greece 3-0 to open Group C on Saturday in front of a big, loud pro-Colombian crowd. Left back Pablo Armero opened the scoring in the fifth minute when his deflected shot rolled past Greece goalkeeper Orestis Karnezis. Striker Teofilo Gutierrez poked in Colombia's second goal from a deflected corner in the 58th and James Rodriguez capped it off with a low shot in stoppage time after a slick backheel flick from Juan Cuadrado. Colombia's pace and trickery were too much for Greece to handle in the latter stages as the 57,174 crowd, most dressed in bright yellow shirts, constantly chanted "Ole, ole" at Estadio Mineriao. "It was a good debut with players who had never played a World Cup. It's a big satisfaction," Colombia coach Jose Pekerman said. "This is how we planned this match. Some things turned out the way we wanted, some things didn't, but we were very focused and showed that we're here to play a good World Cup." Rodriguez said the result showed Colombia was on the right path. "It's a good start, but we have to be calm for what's ahead, which will be even more difficult," he said, looking ahead at Group C matches against Ivory Coast and Japan. Greece had the majority of possession with 54 percent, but whenever Colombia players got hold of the ball, they blazed down the flanks and through the middle of the pitch, disorientating their opponents. The Greeks had a chance to pull a goal back after Gutierrez scored, but an unmarked Georgios Samaras rattled the crossbar from two yards out. There were other chances, too. Panagiotis Kone's shot went just wide in the first half. Colombia goalkeeper David Ospina then made an outstanding reaction save to deny Kone just before halftime. "You can't make excuses when you lose 3-0," Greece coach Fernando Santos said. "We had good spells of play and all we can do now is look at the next matches." Kone said Greece didn't make the most of its possession and the chances it created. "The score doesn't show it, but I think we played well," he said. "But these games are turned on the details, and they didn't work for us today. We're still proud to be here, and we'll continue to play hard." Greece defenders started getting frustrated with Colombia's pace in the second half and began to foul the South Americans, with two players picked up yellow cards within minutes. The result will give Colombia confidence that it can win competitive matches without star striker Radamel Falcao, who was ruled out after failing to recover from a serious knee injury. The other Group C teams, Ivory Coast and Japan, were playing later Saturday. Greece will now have to regroup ahead of its second match against Japan after its notoriously solid defense leaked three goals.

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Colombia 1, Greece 0. Pablo Armero (Colombia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Juan Cuadrado.
Carlos Sánchez (Colombia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Sokratis Papastathopoulos (Greece) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Dimitrios Salpingidis (Greece) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Greece. Giannis Fetfatzidis replaces Dimitrios Salpingidis.
Goal!  Colombia 2, Greece 0. Teófilo Gutiérrez (Colombia) left footed shot from very close range to the top left corner. Assisted by Abel Aguilar   following a corner.
Substitution, Greece. Konstantinos Mitroglou replaces Theofanis Gekas.
Substitution, Colombia. Alexander Mejía replaces Abel Aguilar.
Substitution, Colombia. Santiago Arias replaces Pablo Armero.
Substitution, Colombia. Jackson Martínez replaces Teófilo Gutiérrez.
Substitution, Greece. Giorgos Karagounis replaces Panagiotis Kone.
Goal!  Colombia 3, Greece 0. James Rodríguez (Colombia) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Juan Cuadrado.
